understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of KA59 was to complete a puzzle game by progressing through multiple levels. Each level required moving a pushable object (block) onto a goal tile marked with '+' on the semantic grid. The game ended after 49 turns across multiple levels, with the objective being to satisfy the WIN condition by positioning objects correctly on their designated goal tiles.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based with 3 pixels per cell, controlled via MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN actions that shifted the currently selected piece. The game featured multiple selectable pieces that could be switched using CLICK with target coordinates. Some objects were pushable blocks that would move along with the selected piece if adjacent in the movement direction. Walls blocked movement and had various behaviors that needed to be discovered through interaction. The semantic_grid provided visual feedback where P=selected piece, S=other selectables, B=blocks, #=walls, +=goals, and .=empty cells. Each level had a finite step budget tracked by 'steps_remaining'.

Each level required moving a pushable object (block) onto a goal tile marked with '+' on the semantic grid. The game ended after 49 turns across multiple levels, with the objective being to satisfy the WIN condition by positioning objects correctly on their designated goal tiles.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based with 3 pixels per cell, controlled via MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN actions that shifted the currently selected piece. The game featured multiple selectable pieces that could be switched using CLICK with target coordinates. Some objects were pushable blocks that would move along with the selected piece if adjacent in the movement direction. Walls blocked movement and had various behaviors that needed to be discovered through interaction. The semantic_grid provided visual feedback where P=selected piece, S=other selectables, B=blocks, #=walls, +=goals, and .=empty cells. Each level had a finite step budget tracked by 'steps_remaining'.
